news_summary: Progress in human rights advances with France legalizing assisted dying and the UK proposing a social media curfew to protect teenagers.
news_summary_fr: Les avancées en matière de droits de l'homme se poursuivent : la France a légalisé l'aide à mourir et le Royaume-Uni a proposé d'instaurer un couvre-feu sur les réseaux sociaux afin de protéger les adolescents.
news_summary_es: Se registran avances en materia de derechos humanos: Francia ha legalizado la muerte asistida y el Reino Unido ha propuesto un toque de queda en las redes sociales para proteger a los adolescentes.

articles.title: France's parliament adopts assisted dying law
articles.summary: France on Wednesday moved closer to joining the ranks of countries that guarantee the right to assisted dying, a move championed by President Emmanuel Macron. Macron had promised an assisted dying law when he was re-elected for a second term in 2022, in a change seen as one of the most important social reforms since France allowed same-sex marriage in 2012. If the country's highest constitutional authority approves the legislation, France will join the Netherlands, Belgium, Switzerland and Canada in legalising assisted dying.
articles.content:
France on Wednesday moved closer to joining the ranks of countries that guarantee the right to assisted dying, a move championed by President Emmanuel Macron. Macron had promised an assisted dying law when he was re-elected for a second term in 2022, in a change seen as one of the most important social reforms since France allowed same-sex marriage in 2012. If the country's highest constitutional authority approves the legislation, France will join the Netherlands, Belgium, Switzerland and Canada in legalising assisted dying.

Video by: Luke SHRAGO
articles.source: Health: news, videos, reports and analysis - France 24
articles.reasoning: The article reports on France's parliament adopting a law to guarantee the right to assisted dying, a significant social reform that advances individual autonomy and dignity at the end of life. This change has broad societal impact, aligns with human rights progress, and follows similar legislation in other countries, indicating meaningful policy advancement.
articles.personality_title: France’s parliament approves law to allow assisted dying
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – France has been discussing a law to allow assisted dying, which means helping people who are very sick and want to end their lives in a peaceful way. This topic is important because it deals with personal choice and how people want to live or die with dignity.

**What happened** – On Wednesday, France’s parliament agreed to a new law that would let people choose assisted dying. This law was supported by President Emmanuel Macron, who promised it when he was re-elected in 2022. Now, the law will be reviewed by France’s highest constitutional court before it can take effect.

**Impact** – If the law is approved, France will become one of the few countries like the Netherlands, Belgium, Switzerland, and Canada that allow assisted dying. This is a big change in France’s social rules and shows respect for people’s rights to make decisions about their own lives, especially at the end.

**What’s next step** – The next step is a review by the country’s constitutional authority. If they approve the law, it will officially become part of France’s legal system. This could also influence other countries to consider similar laws.

**One-sentence takeaway** – France is close to legally allowing assisted dying, marking a major step in respecting personal choice and dignity at the end of life.
articles.personality_title_fr: Le Parlement français adopte la loi sur l’aide à mourir
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – La France discute d’une loi pour permettre l’aide à mourir, c’est-à-dire aider les personnes très malades qui souhaitent mettre fin à leurs jours de manière paisible. Ce sujet est important car il touche au choix personnel et à la dignité de la fin de vie.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Mercredi, le Parlement français a adopté une nouvelle loi qui autorise l’aide à mourir. Cette loi a été soutenue par le président Emmanuel Macron, qui l’avait promise lors de sa réélection en 2022. La loi doit maintenant être examinée par la plus haute instance constitutionnelle avant d’entrer en vigueur.

**Impact** – Si la loi est approuvée, la France rejoindra quelques pays comme les Pays-Bas, la Belgique, la Suisse et le Canada qui autorisent l’aide à mourir. C’est un grand changement dans les règles sociales françaises et cela montre le respect du droit des personnes à décider de leur vie, surtout à la fin.

**Prochaine étape** – L’étape suivante est l’examen par l’autorité constitutionnelle. Si elle approuve la loi, elle entrera officiellement dans le droit français. Cela pourrait aussi encourager d’autres pays à envisager des lois similaires.

**Résumé en une phrase** – La France est proche d’autoriser légalement l’aide à mourir, un pas important pour respecter le choix personnel et la dignité en fin de vie.
articles.personality_title_es: El parlamento francés aprueba la ley de ayuda para morir
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Francia ha estado discutiendo una ley para permitir la ayuda para morir, que significa ayudar a personas muy enfermas que quieren terminar sus vidas de manera pacífica. Este tema es importante porque trata sobre la elección personal y cómo las personas quieren vivir o morir con dignidad.

**Qué pasó** – El miércoles, el parlamento francés aprobó una nueva ley que permitiría la ayuda para morir. Esta ley fue apoyada por el presidente Emmanuel Macron, quien la prometió cuando fue reelecto en 2022. Ahora, la ley será revisada por la máxima autoridad constitucional de Francia antes de que pueda aplicarse.

**Impacto** – Si se aprueba la ley, Francia se unirá a pocos países como los Países Bajos, Bélgica, Suiza y Canadá que permiten la ayuda para morir. Este es un gran cambio en las reglas sociales de Francia y muestra respeto por el derecho de las personas a decidir sobre sus propias vidas, especialmente al final.

**Próximo paso** – El siguiente paso es una revisión por la autoridad constitucional del país. Si aprueban la ley, se convertirá oficialmente en parte del sistema legal francés. Esto también podría influir en otros países para que consideren leyes similares.

**Resumen en una frase** – Francia está cerca de permitir legalmente la ayuda para morir, marcando un paso importante para respetar la elección personal y la dignidad al final de la vida.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of a peaceful French countryside sunset with a softly glowing olive branch gently cradling a delicate, translucent hourglass, symbolizing compassionate choice and the passage of time, set against a backdrop of rolling hills and a calm sky in natural, muted earth tones.

articles.title: Midnight social media curfew proposed for UK teens aged 16 and 17
articles.summary: The government says it will improve teenagers' lives but campaigners call it a "missed opportunity".
articles.content:
Older teenagers in the UK will face an overnight social media curfew, the government has announced - though they will be able to opt out of it by changing their account settings.

It would mean apps such as Instagram, TikTok and YouTube being set to be unavailable by default to 16 and 17-year-olds between midnight and 06:00.

The government also wants "addictive" features such as auto-play and infinite scroll to be set to be disabled, saying - combined with the curfew - the measures will improve teenagers' focus, sleep quality and family life.

However, campaigners such as Ellen Roome, who believes her 14-year-old son Jools Sweeney died in an online challenge gone wrong in 2022, does not think the plan goes far enough as teenagers can switch the setting off.

"I just think it's not good enough really just to have a product you can switch off, it's a bit like offering a 17-year-old a bottle of alcohol and then moving it slightly out of arms reach, they can just drag it back in, I really wish they could go stronger and harder on these things," she told BBC Radio 4's Today programme.

The new plans follow the announcement in June that under-16s in the UK would be banned entirely from a range of platforms.

It adds to a complex - and potentially confusing - mix of policies and measures from the government and tech firms intended to keep young people safe online.

They include optional parental controls over children's devices and child-only versions of popular sites such as YouTube.

Lorna Woods, professor of internet law at Essex University, told the BBC she felt the measure was designed to target apprehensions from parents and child safety groups.

"There have been concerns about the way services keep children and young people engaged on their phones for long periods, and that this can impact their sleep, amongst other effects," she said.
articles.reasoning: The UK government's proposal for a social media curfew for older teens aims to improve teenagers' mental health, sleep quality, and family life by limiting exposure to addictive online features. This policy targets a broad public health and safety issue affecting youth well-being, representing a meaningful, tangible action to protect young people online. Although the measure allows opt-out and has critics, it is a significant step in digital child protection and online safety.
articles.personality_title: UK plans social media curfew for 16- and 17-year-olds to improve sleep and focus
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – The UK government is concerned about the impact of social media on teenagers’ sleep and well-being. Many apps have features that keep users scrolling for a long time, which can affect young people’s health.

**What happened** – The government announced a new rule that will automatically block social media apps like Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube for 16- and 17-year-olds between midnight and 6 a.m. Teenagers can choose to turn off this setting if they want. The plan also includes disabling addictive features like auto-play and endless scrolling.

**Impact** – This is the first time older teens will face a default social media curfew in the UK. The goal is to help them get better sleep, concentrate more, and spend more quality time with family. While some people welcome the change, others worry it won’t be strong enough because teens can still switch it off.

**What’s next step** – The government will likely work with social media companies to put these changes in place. They may also keep improving rules to protect younger users and support parents with controls on devices.

**One-sentence takeaway** – The UK’s new social media curfew aims to help older teens sleep better and focus more by limiting late-night app use, but allows them to opt out if they choose.
articles.personality_title_fr: Le Royaume-Uni propose un couvre-feu sur les réseaux sociaux pour les 16-17 ans afin d’améliorer le sommeil
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Le gouvernement britannique s’inquiète des effets des réseaux sociaux sur le sommeil et le bien-être des adolescents. Beaucoup d’applications ont des fonctions qui poussent à rester connectés longtemps, ce qui peut nuire à la santé des jeunes.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Le gouvernement a annoncé une nouvelle règle qui bloquera automatiquement les applications comme Instagram, TikTok et YouTube pour les 16-17 ans entre minuit et 6 heures du matin. Les adolescents pourront désactiver ce réglage s’ils le souhaitent. Le plan prévoit aussi de désactiver des fonctions addictives comme la lecture automatique et le défilement infini.

**Impact** – C’est la première fois que les adolescents plus âgés auront un couvre-feu par défaut sur les réseaux sociaux au Royaume-Uni. L’objectif est de les aider à mieux dormir, se concentrer davantage et passer plus de temps en famille. Certains accueillent cette mesure positivement, d’autres craignent qu’elle ne soit pas assez forte car les jeunes peuvent la désactiver.

**Prochaine étape** – Le gouvernement travaillera probablement avec les entreprises des réseaux sociaux pour mettre ces changements en place. Il pourrait aussi continuer à renforcer les règles pour protéger les plus jeunes et aider les parents avec des contrôles sur les appareils.

**Une phrase clé** – Le nouveau couvre-feu sur les réseaux sociaux au Royaume-Uni vise à aider les adolescents plus âgés à mieux dormir et se concentrer en limitant l’usage des applications la nuit, tout en leur permettant de choisir de le désactiver.
articles.personality_title_es: El Reino Unido propone un toque de queda en redes sociales para jóvenes de 16 y 17 años para mejorar el sueño
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – El gobierno del Reino Unido está preocupado por cómo las redes sociales afectan el sueño y bienestar de los adolescentes. Muchas apps tienen funciones que hacen que los usuarios permanezcan mucho tiempo conectados, lo que puede afectar la salud de los jóvenes.

**Qué pasó** – El gobierno anunció una nueva regla que bloqueará automáticamente aplicaciones como Instagram, TikTok y YouTube para jóvenes de 16 y 17 años entre medianoche y las 6 de la mañana. Los adolescentes pueden elegir desactivar esta opción si quieren. El plan también incluye desactivar funciones adictivas como la reproducción automática y el desplazamiento infinito.

**Impacto** – Es la primera vez que los adolescentes mayores tendrán un toque de queda predeterminado en redes sociales en el Reino Unido. El objetivo es ayudarles a dormir mejor, concentrarse más y pasar más tiempo en familia. Algunas personas apoyan la medida, pero otras temen que no sea suficiente porque los jóvenes pueden apagarla.

**Próximo paso** – El gobierno probablemente trabajará con las empresas de redes sociales para implementar estos cambios. También podría seguir mejorando las reglas para proteger a usuarios más jóvenes y ayudar a los padres con controles en los dispositivos.

**Frase clave** – El nuevo toque de queda en redes sociales en el Reino Unido busca que los adolescentes mayores duerman mejor y se concentren limitando el uso nocturno de apps, pero les permite optar por desactivarlo si quieren.
articles.image_prompt: A cozy bedroom scene at night with a softly glowing smartphone placed on a bedside table, its screen gently dimmed and surrounded by a protective, translucent clock face showing midnight to 6 AM, while a calm owl perches nearby symbolizing watchfulness and restful sleep, all rendered in warm, muted earth tones with delicate brushstrokes.
